capabilities: Buffer client operations to ensure consistency during server round-trips in real-time operational transformation systems.

procedure.action: Initialize an operation buffer with a fixed size limit of 100 operations, using a FIFO queue to manage incoming client operations and prevent overflow using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Buffer each client operation by appending it to the operation buffer; if buffer size exceeds 100, trigger a buffer overflow warning and discard the oldest operation using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Compose new operations by merging buffered operations with a common base state using operational transformation functions to ensure consistency before sending to the server using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Upon receiving server acknowledgment, transform buffered operations by applying server-acknowledged operations to each buffered operation, ensuring all operations are in sync with the server state using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Flush the operation buffer by removing operations that have been acknowledged by the server, ensuring the buffer only contains pending operations using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Procedure for buffering client operations in real-time OT systems to manage server round-trip delays.
title: Buffering Client Operations During Server Round-Trip

failureConditions: Data inconsistency occurs when operations are not buffered properly, causing out-of-order execution and conflicting states.
Buffer overflow when too many operations are queued without server acknowledgment, causing loss of operations and potential data loss.
Delayed client feedback when buffer processing is inefficient, causing user interface lag and poor user experience.
successConditions: operation_order_correctness: assert FIFO order maintained in buffer
acknowledged_operations_flushed: assert buffer contains only unacknowledged operations
